description.undefined: It is intended to formalize and categorize knowledge about the European catalog of high-risk medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices according to their MDR (EU 2017/745) and IVDR (EU 2017/746) regulatory frameworks. The objective of this ontology is to structure and relate the information coming from the EUDAMED public repository covering the entire regulated part of the life cycle of the devices (Manufacturing, risk classification according to regulations and monitoring of their service (incidents and corrective actions).

comment.undefined: Represents a SWRL rule.
The MAN 001 Manufacturer.
Class representing the manufacturer of a medical device. Conceptually aligned with foaf:Organization.
The DEV001 Device.
Class representing a medical device within the surveillance domain. Device identifiers are aligned with dcterms:identifier.
The device status is active implantable.
The device status is active implant.
The device is active now.
The device status is invitro diagnostic.
The device status is implantable.
Device type about interaction with patient (Active,Implantable,invitro,single use, etc.)
Class by the IVDR (EU 2017/746)
Class by the IVDR (EU 2017/746)
Class by the IVDR (EU 2017/746)
Class by the IVDR (EU 2017/746)
Class by the MDR (EU 2017/745)
Class by the MDR (EU 2017/745)
Class by the MDR (EU 2017/745)
Class representing the regulatory risk classification of a device. Modelled as a controlled classification.
Represents a SWRL rule.
The FSCA 001 corrective action.
Class representing corrective actions taken in response to incidents. Descriptions and dates are aligned with Dublin Core terms.
Corrective action open
Corrective action closed
Class representing the status of a corrective action. Modelled as a controlled vocabulary.
Represents a SWRL rule.
Class representing a country associated with reporting or manufacturer location. Conceptually aligned with EU controlled vocabularies for countries.
Incident type representing a serious reportable incident.
Incident type representing a near miss event.
Class representing the type of incident. Modelled as a controlled classification similar to skos:Concept.
Risk category to performance of the device
Risk category referring to failures of the device.
Risk category referring to any product quality defect.
Risk category referring to any patient harm produced
Risk category referring to safety situation of the device
Risk category referring to errors in diagnostics by the device
Class representing the category assigned to a risk. Conceptually aligned with skos:Concept for classification purposes.
The RSK 003 risk.
Class representing a risk associated with a device or incident. Risk descriptions are aligned with dcterms:description.
The INC 001 incident
Class representing an incident involving a medical device. Temporal properties are aligned with dcterms:date.
Represents a SWRL rule.
Represents a SWRL rule.
Not indicate sex
Sex indicate Female
Sex indicate Male
Class representing the sex category of a patient involved in an incident. Modelled as a controlled vocabulary.
store if the device is implantable or not.
Relates an incident to the patient's sex category, represented as a controlled vocabulary.
Identifier of a corrective action. Aligned with dcterms:identifier.
Name of the device. Similar to rdfs:label.
Stores the imdrf code.
Relates an incident to its type, modelled as a controlled classification.
Date when the incident was reported. Aligned with dcterms:date.
Textual description of a risk. Aligned with dcterms:description.
Relates an incident to the associated risk. Each incident is linked to exactly one risk.
Relates a risk to its category. This classification is aligned with controlled vocabularies similar to skos:Concept.
Relates a risk to another risk that it causes. This property is transitive.
Indicates whether a corrective action was taken for the incident.
Name of the manufacturer. Conceptually aligned with foaf:name.
Stores the manufacturer's authorization number.
Identifier of a device. Aligned with dcterms:identifier.

Date when the incident was detected. Aligned with dcterms:date.
Outcome of the incident as a descriptive value.
Relates a device to its device type. This classification enables linking devices with risks.

Stores the type of corrective action type
Stores if the device is Invitro Device or not
Identifier of an incident. Aligned with dcterms:identifier.

Group of countries affected by the corrective action, represented as a group value.

Identifier of a risk. Aligned with dcterms:identifier.
